one_line_ask: Cut six iPhone-shot graded trading-card / hobby-box photos out of their cluttered backgrounds, straighten and tone them, place each on a clean pure-white background, and export a consistent 2048x2048 square set (plus transparent PNG cutouts) named to match the store's Shopify product handles.
brief: SlabVault Cards, an online collectibles store selling graded slabs and sealed hobby product, needs its new-inventory photos turned into clean, upload-ready Shopify images. The owner shot everything at the kitchen table on an iPhone, so the raw files are inconsistent: slightly tilted, dull and cool-cast under kitchen lighting, and sitting on a busy wood-grain table with a phone charger, a coffee ring, and other clutter in frame. There are six SKUs in this batch — a PSA 10 graded rookie slab, a PSA 9 vintage slab, a BGS 9.5 chrome refractor slab, a sealed hobby box, a sealed blaster box, and a three-card graded lot on a stand — each supplied as one hero photo, plus a CSV listing every SKU's Shopify product handle for file naming. The job, applied identically to all six so the storefront looks cohesive: (1) lift the product cleanly off the cluttered table so no wood grain, charger, or coffee ring survives — deliver a transparent PNG cutout of each item, and also place each cutout onto a clean, pure-white (#FFFFFF) background with no drop shadow and no grey gradient, just flat white as Shopify prefers; (2) straighten the tilt so every slab reads dead-level, and auto-tone plus gently wa
